headline: Ranked Oregon seeks consistency against Notre Dame's defense
subhead: Third-ranked Notre Dame hosts a resurgent but uneven Oregon team looking to prove last week's upset of Washington was a turning point.

xFactor.why: Oregon's quarterback will need to sustain drives and avoid the explosive mistakes that have plagued the team through two losses.

keyMatchup: Notre Dame's front four pressure versus Oregon's ability to establish offensive rhythm in the first half will determine field position and momentum.
body: Oregon arrives in South Bend riding genuine momentum off a dramatic road win against Washington, but the underlying tape reveals inconsistency that a Notre Dame defense ranked among the nation's stingiest won't overlook. The Ducks have shown they can execute on the biggest stage, yet a 1-2 record means they cannot afford another letdown against a ranked opponent. Notre Dame dismantled Georgia last week in a statement win, and the coaching room has built a defensive scheme built on gap discipline and tackling precision that will test Oregon's offensive line early and often.

The staff's adjustments to Oregon's pass protection will be critical; if the Ducks can buy time and avoid negative plays on early downs, they have the skill position talent to move the ball. But Notre Dame has allowed just 28 points per game and prides itself on creating third-and-long situations, a formula that has worked against better talent than what Oregon has fielded this season. Expect a low-scoring, field-position battle where field goal efficiency and a single explosive play could determine the outcome.
